note:
随着对执法策略越来越严格的审查，警方使用一种类型的逮捕令来整体监控谷歌用户的位置，这是它面临的第一个法律和政治挑战。
弗吉尼亚州和旧金山的刑事被告正在对“GeoFence”搜查令提出异议，当局可以使用这种搜查令，通过科技公司存储的用户位置历史，扫描地理区域和时间段，寻找嫌疑人。随着这些动议最快在8月份等待辩论和可能的决定，纽约的立法者正在推动立法禁止这种做法。
警方在这两起案件中都求助于Alphabet Google，该公司表示，从2017年到2018年，这类请求的数量增长了1500%，从2018年到2019年，又增长了500%。这家搜索和广告巨头创建了一个特殊的程序来履行逮捕令，但也提出了对执法部门的要求侵犯隐私的担忧。
隐私权倡导者担心，如果更多健身跟踪器、拼车应用和其他公司开始存储用户的精细位置历史，以完善他们的产品和服务，谷歌的合规可能会导致当局从这些公司寻求类似的数据。
“如果我们不取缔这种做法，我们将看到这个不断扩大的地理定位搜查令世界，它将追踪大量的人，”非营利性倡导组织监视技术监督项目(SurveMonitoring Technology Supervisor Project)的执行主任阿尔伯特·福克斯·卡恩(Albert Fox Cahn)说。
巧合的是，在5月份乔治·弗洛伊德(George Floyd)在明尼阿波利斯警方拘留期间被杀后，执法部门受到了批评。
纽约州民主党参议员路易斯·塞普尔韦达(Luis Sepúlveda)是州参议院犯罪受害者、犯罪和矫正委员会主席，他在4月份共同发起了一项法案，宣布逮捕令和非正式要求提供此类数据为非法。塞普尔韦达警告说，与截停搜身等许多其他警察战术一样，在有色人种的社区中，会不成比例地使用地栅栏搜查令。
旧金山公设辩护律师上个月提交了一项动议，要求撤销2019年2月逮捕30岁的拉坎·道斯(Laquan Dawes)时使用的这样一项逮捕令，该逮捕令与该市日落区的一起入室盗窃案有关。他们辩称，当局在两个多小时的时间里，在房子周围半个街区的范围内搜查谷歌用户，最终将矛头对准了道斯，侵犯了第四修正案对不合理搜查和扣押的权利。
另外，在弗吉尼亚州，辩护律师正在寻求在8月举行听证会，代表25岁的奥卡洛·查特里(Okello Chtrie)提出类似的论点。查特里被控武装抢劫里士满郊外的一家银行。
法庭文件显示，这家科技公司创建了一个分三步走的流程，在满足当局的地理围栏搜查令的同时，尽可能多地保持用户隐私。
谷歌搜索其整个账户数据库，启用位置历史记录，以检查哪些用户通过搜查令指定的一般区域和时间段。该公司将这些信息汇编成匿名数据集，供警方使用。
当局审查哪些设备可能是感兴趣的，可能会要求提供有关特定用户的额外位置数据作为上下文。
提交给美国弗吉尼亚州东区地区法院的文件显示，在警方要求提供有关在一小时内经过犯罪现场150米范围内的设备的信息后，谷歌梳理了每一个选择使用位置历史功能的用户，以缩小潜在匹配的范围。
根据谷歌在该案中提交的文件，截至去年，大约三分之一的谷歌用户选择了加入，将与数千万人甚至可能是数亿人有关的信息发送到一个内部称为Sensorvault的数据库中。谷歌的一名代表拒绝提供确切的数字。
法庭文件显示，该公司的律师没有对查特里一案中的逮捕令表明立场。但他们称这一策略是“对隐私的重大侵犯”，同时强调了不准确数据的潜在可能性。该公司发言人拒绝透露收到的认股权证数量。
谷歌建立了一个特殊的协议来处理这些请求。首先，谷歌搜索Sensorvault，检查哪些用户在指定的时间段内通过该区域，向当局提供匿名信息。在警方决定哪些设备是他们感兴趣的之后，他们可能会迫使谷歌识别特定的用户。
谷歌执法和信息安全主管Richard Salgado在一封电子邮件中表示：“我们在大力保护用户隐私的同时，支持执法部门的重要工作。”
Facebook的一位发言人说，Facebook收到了这样的授权，但没有履行这些授权，因为这家社交媒体平台的位置数据不如设备制造商精确，而且数据的存储方式也存在限制。他拒绝解释这些限制。
拼车公司Lyft的一位女发言人说，如果搜查令不针对“大范围内的所有用户”，或者不针对“过长的时间段”等标准，拼车公司Lyft可能会遵守。她没有定义这些术语。
健身跟踪公司Garmin还没有收到这样的请求，也不会满足它们，因为“这将非常侵犯我们用户的隐私权，”一位代表说。
在冠状病毒大流行期间，在纽约禁止此类搜查的法案已经陷入停滞，但塞普尔韦达和州参议院的另一位共同发起人、参议员约翰·刘(John Liu)希望，随着危机的消退，该法案能再次得到讨论。
Facebook发言人表示，该公司一直“支持”该法案，但拒绝详细说明。谷歌的萨尔加多说，公司很高兴看到关于“需要制定规则来规范执法部门对数据的无目标访问”的讨论。
纽约州参议员塞普尔韦达在一次采访中敦促谷歌加大对当局的反击力度。“如果人们意识到发生了什么，他们将损失惨重，”他说。
note_en:
Police use of a type of warrant to monitor Google users’ locations en masse is staring down its first legal and political challenges as scrutiny of law enforcement tactics grows.
  Criminal defendants in Virginia and San Francisco are disputing “geofence” warrants, which authorities can use to scan geographic areas and time periods for suspects through user location histories stored by technology companies. As these motions await arguments and potential decisions as soon as August, New York lawmakers are pushing legislation to ban the practice.
  Police turned in both cases to  Alphabet  Google, which says the number of such requests grew 1,500% from 2017 to 2018, and an additional 500% from 2018 to 2019. The search and advertising giant created a special process to fulfill the warrants but has raised concerns that the demands made by law enforcement invade privacy.
  Privacy advocates fear that compliance by Google could lead authorities to seek similar data from fitness trackers, ride-share apps and other companies if more of those businesses begin to store users’ granular location histories to hone their products and services.
    “We’re going to see this ever-expanding universe of geolocation warrants that will be tracking huge numbers of people if we don’t outlaw the practice,” said Albert Fox Cahn, executive director of the Surveillance Technology Oversight Project, a nonprofit advocacy group.
  The pushback coincidentally comes amid a drumbeat of criticism of law enforcement after the killing of George Floyd in Minneapolis police custody in May.
  New York state Sen. Luis Sepúlveda, a Democrat who chairs the state senate’s Crime Victims, Crime and Correction Committee, co-sponsored a bill in April to outlaw the warrants and informal requests for such data. Mr. Sepúlveda warned that geofence warrants would be used disproportionately in communities of color like many other police tactics, such as stop-and-frisk.
  San Francisco public defenders filed a motion last month to quash such a warrant used in the February 2019 arrest of LaQuan Dawes, 30, in connection with a home burglary in the city’s Sunset District. They argued authorities’ search for Google users within a half-block radius of the house over more than a two-hour period, which eventually zeroed in on Mr. Dawes, violated his Fourth Amendment rights against unreasonable search and seizure.
  Separately in Virginia, defense lawyers are seeking an August hearing to make similar arguments on behalf of Okello Chatrie, 25, who is accused of armed robbery of a bank outside of Richmond.
  The tech company created a three-step process for fulfilling authorities’ geofence warrants while maintaining as much user privacy as possible, according to court documents.
  Google searches its entire database of accounts with location history enabled to check which users passed through the general area and time period specified in a warrant. The company compiles the information into an anonymized data set for police.
  Authorities review which devices may be of interest, potentially asking for additional location data about specific users for context.
  After police requested information about devices that passed within 150 meters of the crime scene over an hourlong period, Google combed through every user who opted into the location-history feature to narrow down potential matches, according to documents filed in the U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of Virginia.
  Roughly one-third of all Google users opted in as of last year, according to the company’s filings in the case, sending information related to tens or potentially hundreds of millions of people to a database known internally as Sensorvault. A Google representative declined to provide precise numbers.
  Lawyers for the company didn’t take a position on the warrant in Mr. Chatrie’s case, according to court documents. But they called the tactic “a significant incursion on privacy” while highlighting a potential for imprecise data. A company spokesman declined to share the number of warrants it receives.
  Google built a special protocol to process the requests. First, Google searches Sensorvault to check which users passed through the area and during the time period specified, providing anonymized information to authorities. After police decide which devices are of interest, they may compel Google to identify specific users.
  “We vigorously protect the privacy of our users while supporting the important work of law enforcement,” Richard Salgado, Google’s director of law enforcement and information security, said in an email.
    Facebook  receives such warrants but doesn’t fulfill them because the social media platform has less-precise location data than device makers and limitations in how the data is stored, a spokesperson said. He declined to explain those limitations.
  Ride-sharing company  Lyft  potentially would comply if warrants don’t target “all users in a large geographical area,” a spokeswoman said, or for “overly broad time periods,” among other criteria. She didn’t define those terms.
  Fitness-tracking firm  Garmin  hasn’t received such requests and wouldn’t fulfill them because “it would be very invasive of our users’ privacy rights,” according to a representative.
    The bill to ban such searches in New York has stalled amid the coronavirus pandemic, but Mr. Sepúlveda and Sen. John Liu, another co-sponsor in the state senate, hope to take it up again as the crisis subsides.
  The Facebook spokesperson said the company has “been supportive” of the bill but declined to elaborate. Google’s Mr. Salgado said the company is encouraged to see a discussion about “the need for rules to govern untargeted access to data by law enforcement.”
  Mr. Sepúlveda, the New York state senator, urged in an interview that Google push back harder against authorities. “They stand to lose a lot if people realize what’s going on,” he said.
title: 警方要求提供谷歌用户位置历史记录面临新的审查-Geofence搜查令正盯着他们的第一个法律挑战
title_en: Police Requests for Google Users’ Location Histories Face New Scrutiny - Geofence warrants are staring down their first legal challenges

note:
大家好，欢迎回到TechCrunch专注于风险投资的播客The Equity，在这里我们解开标题背后的数字。
这是股票星期一，我们每周的开播节目追踪最新的重大新闻，谈论未来一周的情况，深入研究最近几轮融资，并考虑来自私人市场的更大的主题或叙事。你可以在推特上关注这部剧，我也可以在这里关注，别忘了看看上周五的那一集。
正如你可能预料到的那样，关于TikTok和微软的合作，我们有很多话要说，但不知何故，这一合作仍在进行中。其他事情也发生了，别担心。以下是概要：
洛德斯敦汽车公司(Lordstown Motors)正寻求通过SPAC上市。对于这一点，我们不得不说，电动汽车的繁荣和SPAC的崩溃将会融合在一起，并让一些人损失很多钱。不一定是这笔交易，别介意。
而Zoom关于中国市场的最新举措感觉就像是即将到来的时代的预兆。
在TikTok方面，微软从未真正完全放弃消费者硬件和软件，只是在现任首席执行官萨蒂亚·纳德拉(Satya Nadella)的领导下进行了深度修剪。Windows Phone？不见了。浮出水面？比以往任何时候都要大。搅拌机？不是的。宾？是啊。诸如此类的事情。微软，就像任何现代的超级平台一样，不仅仅想在你工作的时候占有你的时间。它想全天候烧坏你的眼睛。
我们认为，对于Yuri Milner、红杉资本中国(Sequoia Capital China)、通用大西洋(General Atlantic)、软银(SoftBank)、高盛(Goldman Sachs)和摩根士丹利(Morgan Stanley)等ByteDance的众多支持者来说，这笔交易可能相当有利可图。
最后，微软为什么要收购TikTok？我们有很多想法，所有这些想法都可以归结为可能的竞购者，但当我们浏览了可能的追求者-特朗普风暴中的港口-微软可能比我们想象的更有意义？
不管是哪种情况，我们都迫不及待地要等到萨蒂亚穿着傻乎乎的衣服一边跳舞一边指着屏幕上的文字宣布交易。
股市每周一上午7点下跌。PT和周五上午6点。PT，所以在Apple播客、阴天、Spotify和所有演员身上订阅我们吧。
note_en:
Hello and welcome back to  Equity, TechCrunch’s venture capital-focused podcast where we unpack the numbers behind the headlines.
 This is  Equity   Monday, our weekly kickoff that tracks the latest big news, chats about the coming week, digs into some recent funding rounds and mulls over a larger theme or narrative from the private markets. You can follow the show on Twitter  here, and myself  here, and don’t forget to check out  last Friday’s episode.
 As you probably expected, we had a lot to say about the  TikTok  -Microsoft tie-up that is somehow still afoot. Other things happened too, don’t worry. Here’s the rundown:
 Lordstown Motors is  looking to go public via a SPAC. To which we have to say that the EV boom and SPAC crush are going to fuse and lose some people a lot of money. Not this deal, necessarily, mind.
  And Zoom’s  latest move regarding the Chinese market feels like a harbinger of times to come.
 On the TikTok front, Microsoft never really fully abandoned consumer hardware and software, it just pruned deeply under its current CEO Satya Nadella. Windows Phone? Gone. Surface? Bigger than ever. Mixer? No. Bing? Yep. That sort of thing. And Microsoft, like any modern super-platform, doesn’t just want to own your time when you are at work. It wants to burn your eyes out around the clock.
 For a  host of ByteDance backers like Yuri Milner, Sequoia Capital China, General Atlantic, SoftBank, and Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley, the deal could be rather lucrative, we presume.
  Finally,  why does Microsoft want to buy TikTok? We had a number of ideas that all sort of summed to  maybe, but when we ran through the big tech companies that were possible suitors — ports in the Trump storm — maybe Microsoft makes more sense than we would have guessed?
 Whatever the case, we can’t wait until Satya announces the deal by dancing and pointing at text on a screen while wearing something silly.
 Equity drops every Monday at 7:00 a.m. PT and Friday at 6:00 a.m. PT, so subscribe to us on   Apple Podcasts ,   Overcast ,   Spotify  and all the casts.
title: 股票周一：Satya和TikTok能让字节跳动的投资者高兴得手舞足蹈吗？
title_en: Equity Monday: Could Satya and TikTok make Bytedance investors happy enough to dance?

note:
微软周日宣布，正在讨论收购美国、加拿大、澳大利亚和新西兰的TikTok。
在微软周日证实正在讨论收购美国、加拿大、澳大利亚和新西兰的TikTok之后，人们开始质疑TikTok在欧洲的未来。
TikTok的母公司ByteDance总部设在北京，在唐纳德·特朗普总统表示他打算在美国禁止这款视频分享应用后，该公司正被迫考虑出售，因为他担心它可能会与中国政府共享数据。TikTok一再否认这些指控。
微软在一份声明中表示，它将继续与美国政府合作达成协议，并打算在9月15日之前结束谈判。目前还不清楚微软准备支付多少钱，TikTok拒绝对谈判发表评论。
如果交易成功，ByteDance能否继续在欧洲运营TikTok？上海咨询公司AgencyChina的研究和战略经理迈克尔·诺里斯(Michael Norris)认为是这样的。周一，他告诉CNBC，TikTok在欧洲的业务似乎不在谈判桌上。在这个阶段，字节跳动似乎对TikTok在欧洲市场的运营能力很有信心。
如果交易成功，微软将在4个国家运营TikTok，而ByteDance将在其他100多个国家运营。诺里斯说，同样的产品在不同的司法管辖区由不同的各方运营，这似乎是史无前例的。这些考虑因素，包括合规的运营模式以及相关的技术、基础设施和准入转让，将使收购谈判复杂化。
虽然欧洲各国政府提出了一些担忧，但他们还没有说要禁止TikTok。根据应用程序追踪公司Sensor Tower的数据，TikTok在过去几年里大受欢迎，在全球范围内累计下载量超过20亿次。
一位英国政府发言人说：“我们总是采取措施，确保用户受到适当的保护，免受公司滥用或利用他们个人数据的企图。”
TikTok受GDPR规定的数据隐私规则约束。这对所有收集和处理个人数据的移动应用提供商来说都是一样的。
关于我们更广泛的在线危害工作，我们正在制定一些世界上最严格的法律，以使英国成为一个更安全的在线地方。这将赋予社交媒体平台在独立监管机构监督下管理个人数据的谨慎义务。
为了与北京保持距离，并安抚西方国家，TikTok在中国出台新的全面国家安全法后撤出了香港。
TikTok还聘请了一名美国首席执行官，并在世界各地设立了当地办事处。上个月，该公司表示，希望未来三年在美国招聘1万名员工，目前已经从谷歌和Facebook招聘了几名关键高管。
ByteDance还计划在中国以外的地方为TikTok设立一个新的国际总部。问题是，它会在哪里呢？TikTok最大的办公室所在的洛杉矶显然是领跑者，但即将出台的美国禁令和潜在的微软交易让事情变得复杂起来。
据英国小报“太阳报”报道，TikTok目前正计划将伦敦作为其国际总部所在地。据报道，字节跳动的创始人张一鸣和TikTok的创始人亚历克斯·朱可能会搬到伦敦。
周日晚些时候，字节跳动(ByteDance)表示，它致力于成为一家全球性公司，但根据CNBC翻译的一份公司声明，它面临着各种复杂而不可想象的困难。
像Facebook和Alphabet的YouTube这样的美国科技巨头直接与TikTok竞争，两家公司之间的紧张关系一直在加剧。周一，字节跳动指控Facebook抄袭和诽谤，但没有详细说明。
从数据存储到算法，TikTok的大部分工作原理都笼罩在神秘之中，但该公司上周承诺变得更加透明，并敦促其他公司也这样做。
note_en:
Microsoft announced Sunday it is in discussions to buy TikTok in the U.S., Canada, Australia, and New Zealand.
 Questions are being asked about TikTok's future in Europe after  Microsoft confirmed Sunday it is in discussions to buy TikTok in the U.S., Canada, Australia, and New Zealand.
 TikTok parent company ByteDance, which is headquartered in Beijing, is being forced to consider a sale after President Donald Trump said he intends to ban the video sharing app in the U.S. over fears that it could share data with the Chinese government. TikTok has repeatedly denied the allegations.
  Microsoft  said in a statement that it will keep working with the U.S. government on a deal and that it intends to conclude talks by September 15. It's unclear how much Microsoft is prepared to pay and TikTok declined to comment on the discussions.
 If the deal were to go through, would ByteDance be able to continue operating TikTok across Europe? Michael Norris, a research and strategy manager at Shanghai-based consultancy AgencyChina, thinks so. "TikTok's business in Europe doesn't appear to be on the table," he told CNBC Monday. "At this stage, it looks like ByteDance is confident in TikTok's ability to operate in European markets."
 If the deal goes through, Microsoft would operate TikTok in four countries while ByteDance would operate it in over 100 other countries. "The same product operated by different parties in different jurisdictions seems unprecedented," Norris said. "These considerations, including compliant operating models and relevant transfers of technology, infrastructure, and access, will complicate acquisition negotiations."
 While European governments have raised some concerns, they haven't gone as far as saying they'll ban TikTok, which has become wildly popular in the last couple of years, racking up over 2 billion downloads worldwide, according to app tracker Sensor Tower.
 A U.K. government spokesperson said: "We always take steps to ensure users are properly protected from attempts by companies to misuse or exploit their personal data."
  "TikTok is subject to the data privacy rules set out under GDPR. This is the same for all mobile app providers that collect and process personal data."
 "In relation to our wider online harms work, we are developing some of the most stringent laws in the world to make the U.K. a safer place to be online. These will put a duty of care on social media platforms to manage people's personal data, overseen by an independent regulator."
 In a bid to distance itself from Beijing and reassure Western nations, TikTok  withdrew from Hong Kong after China introduced new sweeping national security laws.
 TikTok has also hired an  American chief executive and set up local offices around the world. Last month, the company said it  wanted to hire 10,000 staff in the U.S. over the next three years, and it has already  hired several key executives from Google and Facebook.
  ByteDance is also planning to set up a new international headquarters for TikTok outside of China. The question is, where will it be? Los Angeles, which is home to TikTok's biggest office, was the obvious front-runner but the impending U.S. ban and the potential Microsoft deal complicate matters.
  According to a  report from British tabloid newspaper The Sun, TikTok is now planning to make London the home of its international headquarters. The move could reportedly see ByteDance's founder Zhang Yiming and TikTok's creator Alex Zhu relocate to London.
  Late Sunday,  ByteDance said that it is "committed to becoming a global company" but it has faced "all kinds of complex and unimaginable difficulties," according to a CNBC translation of a company statement.
 U.S. tech giants like Facebook and Alphabet's YouTube compete directly with TikTok and tensions have been growing between the firms. On Monday, ByteDance accused Facebook of "plagiarism and smear" without elaborating.
 Much of how TikTok works, from its data storage to its algorithms have been shrouded in mystery but the company pledged to  become more transparent last week and it urged others to do the same.
title: CNBC

note:
谷歌发布了一款Chrome扩展，可以让你更深入地了解你是如何成为广告商的目标的。它被称为“广告透明聚焦”，它显示一个页面上加载的广告数量、广告客户和广告技术公司，以及个人数据(人口统计数据、再营销、兴趣、位置等)。这是用来为广告服务的。
谷歌在其Chrome网上商店的描述中写道：“我们新的Chrome广告透明聚光灯(Alpha)扩展是我们正在进行的计划的一部分，目的是让人们更清楚地了解用于个性化广告的数据，并更好地控制这些数据。”
谷歌指出，这个早期版本只显示了关于谷歌服务的广告的信息，这些广告已经实现了谷歌的“广告披露模式”，而不是其他第三方广告。根据谷歌的Github页面，这个方案的设计是为了帮助所有的广告商披露他们是如何使用你的个人信息的。谷歌表示：“随着时间的推移，我们希望该行业的其他公司能将广告披露模式纳入他们的广告中。”
除了显示广告信息，该扩展还显示“所有在页面上存在的公司和服务”，包括内容交付网络或分析提供商。它提供了指向每家公司隐私政策的链接，显示了每家公司是如何收集和存储用户数据的。请注意，这个扩展在美国以外的地区可能会有不同的效果-我们中的几个欧洲人可以看到广告商和广告数量，但不能看到任何个人跟踪信息。
这一延期是谷歌提高广告透明度的努力的一部分，也可能是为了避免欧盟GDPR法律等进一步以隐私为导向的立法。与此同时，谷歌正试图通过逐步淘汰第三方cookie和引入新的广告拦截技术来取代目前狂野的西部广告系统，这种技术可以过滤行为不端的广告，同时让好的广告通过。与此同时，为了帮助依赖广告的网站，谷歌还对Chrome进行了一系列修改，以阻止广告拦截程序有效工作。
新的扩展很有帮助，但Ghostery等其他扩展提供了关于跟踪器、广告和其他信息的更详细信息，同时也让你能够屏蔽它们。另一个选项是Brave Browser，它还可以显示和阻止跟踪器信息，同时让您可以访问更私密的浏览选项。
note_en:
Google has released a Chrome extension that can give you more insight into how you’re being targeted by advertisers. Called “ Ads Transparency Spotlight,” it shows the number of ads loading on a page, the advertisers and ad tech companies present, and the personal data (demographics, re-marketing, interests, location, etc.) that is being used to serve the ads.
 “Our new Ads Transparency Spotlight (Alpha) extension for Chrome is part of our ongoing initiative to give people more visibility into the data used to personalize ads and more control over that data,” Google wrote in the description on its Chrome web store.
  Google notes that this early version only shows information about Google-served ads that have implemented Google’s “ Ad Disclosure Schema,” and not other third-party ads. That schema is desiggned to help all of its advertisers disclose how they’re using your personal information, according to Google’s  Github page. ”Over time, we hope the [rest of] the industry will incorporate the Ad Disclosure Schema into their ads,” Google said.
 On top of showing ad information, the extension shows “all companies and services with a presence on the page,” including content delivery networks or analytics providers. It provides a link to each company’s privacy policy, showing how each collects and stores user data. Note that the extension may work differently in regions outside the US — several of us in Europe could see the advertisers and number of ads, but not any personal tracking info.
 The extension is part of a push by Google to increase ad transparency and perhaps stave off further privacy-oriented legislation like the EU’s  GDPR laws. At the same time, Google is trying to replace the current wild west ad system by gradually  phasing out third-party cookies and introducing new  ad-blocking tech that filters misbehaving ads while letting good ones through. At the same time, it introduced a suite  changes to Chrome that would stop ad-blockers from working effectively, in order to help sites that rely on advertising.
 The new extension is helpful, but others like  Ghostery offer more detailed information about trackers, ads and other info, while giving you the ability to block them as well. Another option is the  Brave browser, which also displays and blocks tracker information, while giving you access to more private browsing options.
title: 谷歌最新的Chrome扩展显示了详细的广告跟踪数据
title_en: Google's latest Chrome extension shows detailed ad-tracking data

note:
作为Y Combinator最新团队的一员，OneKey计划将键盘转变为一种新的方式，提供日历、待办事项列表，甚至Salesforce功能，从而使移动设备上的工作变得更容易。
巴雷说，人们有用于表情符号、其他语言和gif的键盘，但将商业应用程序集成到键盘功能中的方法很少。他想要改变这一点。
目前，该公司的第一个把戏将是在键盘界面上安装一个类似Calendly的日程安排应用程序。随着时间的推移，该公司将寻求创建可以在应用商店风格的市场上销售的模块，用于智能手机的键盘空间。
对巴尔来说，OneKey背后的灵感来自于在拉丁美洲工作的时间，以及主要通过WhatsApp开展业务的时间。该工具非常适用于消息传递，但企业功能在日程安排或其他企业应用程序集成方面出现了问题。
巴尔说：“人们在移动设备上做的事情越来越多，现在商业上也是如此。”“当你从以电脑为基础的世界切换到移动电话时，很多生产力功能就消失了。”
巴尔来自巴黎郊区，和他的伴侣一起去了波哥大。她住在那里，而他正在为一家名为DeepLook的销售自动化初创公司工作。巴尔和他的DeepLook联合创始人(也是他的高中朋友)尤利西斯·普雷吉尔(Ulysses Pryjiel)一起着手研究是否可以将他需要的一些商业工具带到移动环境中。
对Barre来说，最大的认识是手机上键盘输入所在的空间没有得到充分利用。他认为OneKey是一种手机浏览器扩展，集中在键盘领域。
“应用程序的市场是长远的愿景，”Barre说。“这就是你如何为人们带来越来越多的价值。我们一开始就使用日历和列表等功能，这些功能可以快速带来更多价值，而不会过于专门化。“。
这个想法并不完全是新奇的。Barre说，SwiftKey有一个墙纸市场，但没有他设想的应用程序和服务类型那么强大。
巴尔说：“如果你可以在普通的应用程序中做到这一点，那么很有可能你也可以通过键盘来做到这一点。”
note_en:
A member of  Y Combinator’s   most recent cohort, OneKey has a plan to make work easier on mobile devices by turning the keyboard into a new way to serve up applications like calendars, to-do lists, and, eventually, even  Salesforce   functionality.
 People have keyboards for emojis, other languages, and gifs, but there have been few ways to integrate business apps into the keyboard functionality, says Barre. And he’s out to change that.
  Right now, the company’s first trick will be getting a Calendly-like scheduling app onto the keyboard interface. Over time, the company will look to create modules that they can sell in an app-store style marketplace for the keyboard space on smartphones.
  For Barre, the inspiration behind OneKey was the time spent working in Latin America and primarily conducting business through WhatsApp. The tool was great for messaging, but enterprise functionality broke down across for scheduling or other enterprise app integrations.
 “People are doing more and more stuff on mobile and it’s happening right now in business,” said Barre. “When you switch from a computer-based world to a mobile phone, a lot of the productivity features disappear.”
 Barre, originally from the outskirts of  Paris,   traveled to Bogota with his partner. She was living there and he was working on a sales automation startup called DeepLook. Together with his DeepLook co-founder (and high school friend), Ulysses Pryjiel, Barre set out to see if he could bring some of the business tools he needed over to the mobile environment.
 The big realization for Barre was the under-utilized space on the phone where the keyboard inputs reside. He thinks of OneKey as a sort of browser extension for mobile phones, centered in the keyboard real estate.
 “The marketplace for apps is the longterm vision,” said Barre. “That’s how you bring more and more value to people. We started with those features like calendars and lists that brought more value quickly without being too specialized.”
 The idea isn’t entirely novel. SwiftKey had a marketplace for wallpapers, Barre said, but nothing as robust as the kinds of apps and services that he envisions.
 “If you can do it in a regular app, it’s very likely that you can do it through a keyboard,” Barre said.
title: OneKey通过将应用程序集成到移动键盘中，使无需桌面即可轻松工作
title_en: OneKey makes it easier to work without a desktop by integrating apps into mobile keyboards

note:
跳到主要内容乍一看，微软收购TikTok似乎有点不寻常。多年来，微软一直在放弃Groove Music服务、Kinect Xbox配件、Microsoft Band健身设备、Windows Phone等消费者产品，最近又推出了Mixer流媒体服务。微软一直青睐其企业软件和服务，甚至Cortana也转变为以生产力为中心。一项面向跳舞青少年的服务如何与微软保守的商业人群相契合呢？
然而，如果你更深入地挖掘微软未来的雄心，收购TikTok在美国、加拿大、澳大利亚和新西兰的业务可能会让微软的许多现有业务受益，同时也会使该公司成为YouTube和Facebook的真正竞争对手。
任何TikTok交易的关键部分都将是微软获得的数据和用户。这就是特朗普政府对TikTok与中国政府的潜在联系以及这些数据可能被滥用的担忧背后的推动力。微软在其确认收购谈判的博客文章中承认了这些数据的重要性，并指出“微软将确保TikTok美国用户的所有私人数据都转移到美国，并保留在美国。”
微软可以通过多种方式使用这些数据。这家软件巨头长期以来一直使用Xbox Live为微软研究院(Microsoft Research)的部分软件和硬件项目提供燃料，这些使用数据有助于游戏开发商和微软更好地了解人们是如何使用Xbox的。了解人们是如何与Xbox上的Kinect附件互动和使用的，最终也帮助微软开发和改进了HoloLens。
TikTok可以帮助纠正微软的一个盲点，甚至可以影响公司内部其他软件和服务的开发方式。微软拥有软件商业使用所需的所有数据，但近年来在纯消费者服务方面并不成功，这使得该公司对消费者行为的洞察力存在差距。
当你考虑到大量的美国年轻人是在教室里由Android、iOS和Chromebook主导的环境中长大的，这一点尤其相关。由于Gmail还主导着消费者的电子邮件使用和通过Google Docs的文档共享，在美国成长为可能的人不需要任何微软软件或服务。微软错过了移动革命，从那以后一直在追赶，但它不想错过整整一代未来的员工。
TikTok为微软提供了一条直接联系数百万使用该应用程序观看视频的年轻人，甚至是那些使用它创建内容的年轻人的电话。微软不顾一切地试图调整其Windows操作系统，使其通过视频创建应用程序对消费者更加友好，但TikTok为数百万人提供了一种从手机创建视频的简单方式。
微软可以利用这种直接接触TikTok用户的机会，为Surface、Xbox和其他产品投放广告，甚至可以将其作为其游戏流媒体雄心的另一个基础。谷歌正计划利用YouTube整合其Stadia流媒体服务，TikTok将以XCloud游戏流媒体回应微软。微软一直计划将Mixer用于Xbox游戏流媒体，但该服务从未获得足够的吸引力，该公司被迫与Facebook就XCloud集成达成协议。不难想象，在TikTok上观看“使命召唤”视频后，当游戏通过微软的XCloud服务流到你的手机上时，你可以点击并立即玩游戏。
微软在人工智能方面也有远大的雄心，不仅仅是在工作场所。虽然微软最初进军面向消费者的人工智能聊天机器人并没有按计划进行，但微软确实需要一个消费者试验场来开展超越Office的人工智能工作。TikTok已经通过该应用程序广受欢迎的过滤器和驱动For You Feed的推荐引擎，将人工智能用于面部识别。TikTok的AI Feed准确地指示了你在应用程序中看到的内容，而且使用TikTok的人越多，算法就会越好。
TikTok也一直在进军增强现实领域，滤镜和广告都采用了增强现实。微软的AR野心在很大程度上仅限于其HoloLens硬件、Windows混合现实耳机，以及在移动设备上进行的一些带有“我的世界”的试验。TikTok将成为微软进入AR移动世界的另一个门户。
微软没有提供任何关于它的收购将如何直接影响TikTok功能的线索，只是指出它“将建立在TikTok用户目前喜欢的体验上”。目前还不清楚微软如何在美国、加拿大、澳大利亚和新西兰运营TikTok。这使得世界上的大部分地区，特别是亚洲和欧洲的TikTok版本，可能会有与微软版本不同的功能。
如果微软能够获得运营控制权，那么如何在美国和其他地区运营TikTok将是其未来成功的关键。微软首席执行官萨蒂亚·纳德拉(Satya Nadella)的首批大型收购之一是“我的世界”制造商Mojang。它取得了巨大的成功，特别是在“我的世界”持续增长的情况下，微软基本上已经离开了Mojang工作室，继续独立开发这款游戏。
同样，纳德拉以262亿美元收购LinkedIn也是微软的成功案例。该公司一直在单独运营LinkedIn，并在一些有意义的地方巧妙地与Office进行了整合。微软获得了一个庞大的企业社交网络，以抵御来自谷歌和Facebook在工作场所的竞争，因此该公司继续以Office占据主导地位。
即使是GitHub对微软来说也似乎是一笔成功的收购。这家软件制造商也一直在单独运营这项业务，但对于微软来说，了解开发者如何应对世界上的应用需求是一个关键的数据点。
如果微软的TikTok交易成功，那么该公司很可能也会单独运营。微软对Skype和诺基亚手机业务的混乱收购给我们上了一课，告诉我们如何不积极整合业务。微软在内容审核领域也是个新手，因此微软不太可能试图深度整合TikTok或以任何方式重新塑造它的品牌，因此微软不太可能尝试深度整合TikTok或以任何方式重新塑造它的品牌。在英语国家收购TikTok也有助于避免Facebook和Twitter面临的一些适度复杂性。奇怪的是，微软并没有将TikTok的英国业务列为收购目标。(英国、美国、新西兰、澳大利亚和加拿大也是五眼联盟下的情报共享伙伴。)。
单独运行TikTok可以让微软利用其至关重要的数据和集成点，同时也可以将TikTok定位为微软一直想要的YouTube和Facebook竞争对手。早在2006年，微软就与新闻集团(News Corporation)和NBC环球(NBC Universal)联手推出了MSN视频上的Soapbox服务。它未能与YouTube竞争，并在几年后被关闭，留下微软采用YouTube作为其分享自己视频的主要方式。
早在2012年，微软也曾试验过自己的社交网络Socl，但五年后关闭了这项服务。早在2007年，微软最初向Facebook投资2.4亿美元后，就一直意识到Facebook的增长潜力。TikTok正在迅速转变为下一个大型消费者社交媒体空间，成为Snapchat、Instagram和Facebook的直接竞争对手，无论是规模还是分享和评论视频的功能都是如此。
很难说微软潜在的TikTok收购将如何进展。对于该公司来说，这是一笔不同寻常的交易，特别是在美国政府持续威胁的情况下。微软通常不会在交易达成前确认正在进行收购谈判，该公司零星的博客帖子特别感谢特朗普总统的亲自参与。“华尔街日报”报道说，微软的博客帖子可能是由于总统的盟友向该公司施压，要求其公开承认这笔交易。
这是一笔复杂的交易，微软被困在其中。拥有该网络可能会让该公司受益，但如果发生这种情况，也会让特朗普总统受益，他可以声称已经解除了中国在美国科技生态系统中最粘性的立足点之一。他也不会羞于强调他的参与。
未来几周，TikTok谈判可能会有更多波折，但既然微软已承诺不迟于9月15日完成这些讨论，那么TikTok-ing的时间就到了。
note_en:
Skip to main content                                                                                                                At first glance, a Microsoft acquisition of TikTok seems a little unusual. Microsoft has spent years walking back consumer plays like the  Groove Music service, the  Kinect Xbox accessory, its  Microsoft Band fitness device,  Windows Phone, and more recently the  Mixer streaming service. Microsoft has been favoring its enterprise software and services, and even Cortana has  transitioned to be productivity-focused. How does a service that caters to dancing teenagers fit with Microsoft’s buttoned-up business demographic?
 If you dig a little deeper into Microsoft’s future ambitions, though, a move to acquire TikTok’s operations in the US, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand could benefit many of Microsoft’s existing businesses while also setting the company up as a real competitor to YouTube and Facebook.
   The key part of any TikTok deal will be the data and users Microsoft gains access to. This is the driving force behind concerns from the Trump administration over TikTok’s potential ties to the Chinese government and how that data might be misused. Microsoft acknowledges the data’s importance in its  blog post confirming acquisition talks, noting that “Microsoft would ensure that all private data of TikTok’s American users is transferred to and remains in the United States.”
 This data could be used by Microsoft in a variety of ways. The software giant has long used Xbox Live to fuel  parts of Microsoft Research for future software and hardware projects, and the usage data helps game developers and  Microsoft better understand how people use their Xbox. Understanding how people interacted with and used the Kinect accessory for the Xbox ultimately helped Microsoft develop and improve HoloLens, too.
 TikTok could help correct a Microsoft blindspot and even influence how other software and services are developed inside the company. Microsoft has all the data it needs on business usage of software, but it hasn’t been successful with pure consumer services in recent years, which has left the company with a gap of insight into consumer behaviors.
 That’s particularly relevant when you consider that a large number of young Americans are growing up in an environment dominated by Android, iOS, and Chromebooks in classrooms. With Gmail also dominating consumer email usage and document sharing through Google Docs, it’s possible to grow up in the US without needing any Microsoft software or services. Microsoft missed the mobile revolution and has been playing catch-up ever since, but it doesn’t want to miss an entire generation of future workers.
 TikTok gives Microsoft a direct line to millions of youngsters using the app to watch videos and even those who use it to create content. Microsoft has tried desperately to adapt its Windows operating system to be more  consumer-friendly with video creation apps, but TikTok offers an easy way for millions to create videos from their phones instead.
  Microsoft could take advantage of that direct access to TikTok users with ads for Surface, Xbox, and other products, or even as another base for its game-streaming ambitions. Google is planning to leverage YouTube to integrate its Stadia streaming service, and TikTok would give Microsoft a response with xCloud game streaming. Microsoft had been planning to use Mixer for Xbox game streaming, but the service never gained enough traction, and the company was forced to  strike a deal with Facebook for xCloud integration instead. It’s not hard to imagine watching a  Call of Duty video on TikTok and then being able to click and instantly play the game as it streams to your phone via Microsoft’s xCloud service.
 Microsoft also has broad ambitions for artificial intelligence that go beyond just the workplace. While its initial foray into  AI-powered chatbots for consumers didn’t go to plan, Microsoft does need a consumer testing ground for its AI work that goes beyond Office. TikTok already utilizes AI for facial recognition with the app’s popular filters and in the recommendation engine that drives the For You feed. TikTok’s AI feed dictates exactly what you see in the app, and the algorithm improves the more people use TikTok.
 TikTok has also been venturing into augmented reality, with both filters and ads that utilize AR. Microsoft’s AR ambitions have been largely limited to its HoloLens hardware, Windows Mixed Reality headsets, and some  experiments on mobile with  Minecraft. TikTok would be another gateway into the mobile world of AR for Microsoft.
   Microsoft doesn’t offer any clues as to how its acquisition could directly affect TikTok features, apart from noting it “would build on the experience TikTok users currently love.” Exactly how Microsoft could operate TikTok in the US, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand is unclear. This leaves large swathes of the world, particularly across Asia and Europe, with a version of TikTok that will potentially have different features to Microsoft’s version.
 How Microsoft operates TikTok in the US and beyond will be key to its future success if the company is able to acquire operational control. One of Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella’s first big acquisitions was   Minecraft maker Mojang. It has been a huge success, particularly as  Minecraft has continued to grow and Microsoft has largely left the Mojang studio to continue to develop the game independently.
 Equally, Nadella’s acquisition of  LinkedIn for $26.2 billion has also been a success story for Microsoft. The company has been operating LinkedIn separately, with some clever  integration points with Office where it makes sense. Microsoft has access to a giant enterprise social network to  fend off competition from Google and Facebook in the workplace, and the company continues to dominate with Office as a result.
 Even GitHub appears to be a successful  acquisition for Microsoft. The software maker has also been operating this business separately, but again, it’s a key point of data for Microsoft to understand how developers are responding to the world’s app needs.
 If Microsoft’s TikTok deal is successful, then it’s likely the company will also be run separately. Microsoft’s  messy acquisitions of Skype and  Nokia’s phone business have been lessons in how not to aggressively integrate businesses. Microsoft is also new to the world of content moderation and the associated headaches that brings, so it’s unlikely Microsoft will attempt to deeply integrate TikTok or re-brand it in any way. Acquiring TikTok in English-speaking countries also helps avoid some of the moderation complexity faced by Facebook and Twitter. Oddly, Microsoft doesn’t list TikTok’s UK operation as a target for acquisition. (The UK, US, New Zealand, Australia, and Canada are also intelligence sharing partners under the Five Eyes alliance.)
 Running TikTok separately could allow Microsoft to leverage its all-important data and integration points but also position TikTok as the YouTube and Facebook rival Microsoft has always wanted. Microsoft teamed up with News Corporation and NBC Universal back in 2006 to launch its Soapbox on MSN Video service. It failed to compete with YouTube and was shut down a few years later, leaving Microsoft to adopt YouTube as the primary way it shares its own videos.
 Microsoft also experimented with its own social network,  Socl, back in 2012 before  shutting down the service five years later. Microsoft always understood the potential for Facebook’s growth, after initially  investing $240 million in Facebook back in 2007. TikTok is rapidly turning into the next big consumer social media space, acting as a direct competitor to Snapchat, Instagram, and Facebook, both in terms of scale and the features involved in sharing and commenting on videos.
  It’s difficult to say exactly how Microsoft’s potential TikTok takeover will progress. It’s an unusual deal for the company, particularly with the US government’s  ongoing threats. Microsoft doesn’t typically confirm it’s in acquisition talks before deals are made, and the  company’s odd blog post specifically thanks President Trump for his personal involvement.   The Wall Street Journal reports that Microsoft’s blog post may have been prompted specifically by allies of the president pressuring the company to acknowledge the deal publicly.
 It’s a complex deal, and Microsoft is trapped in it. Owning the network could benefit the company, but if it happens, it would also benefit President Trump, who could claim to have unwound one of China’s stickiest footholds in the US tech ecosystem. And he wouldn’t be shy about highlighting his involvement.
 There will likely be more twists and turns in the TikTok talks in the coming weeks, but now that Microsoft has committed to finishing these discussions no later than September 15th, the clock is TikTok-ing.
title: 边缘

note:
现在，来自Y Combinator夏季团队Kuleana的一家初创公司希望它能在这些水域游泳。
虽然像Wild Type，Finless Foods和Shiok Meats这样的新企业都在开发基于细胞的替代品，以取代使用活金枪鱼、鲑鱼和虾，以及Good Catch和Ocean Hugger正在提出自己的金枪鱼替代品，但Kuleana希望通过复制寿司级的生金枪鱼来与众不同。
Kuleana联合创始人兼首席执行官雅克·普鲁斯表示，这只是个开始。最终，该公司希望制造金枪鱼、三文鱼以及更多鱼类和贝类的素食替代品。
“还没有人真正把生金枪鱼做得很好，”雅塞克·普鲁斯(Jacek Prus)说。“我们要做生金枪鱼，然后可能做鲑鱼。”
Prus在德克萨斯大学奥斯汀分校上了一堂动物伦理学课后，开始对食品业感兴趣。在这座城市生活了五年后，普鲁斯搬到了欧洲，并帮助建立了普罗维格国际孵化器。
就是在那里，Prus与Ron Shigeta取得了联系，Ron Shigeta是食品科学领域的长期研究员、技术员和企业家，曾在IndieBio担任首席科学官。Shigeta让Prus与巴塞罗那的食品科学研究员索尼娅·赫塔多(Sonia Hurtado)和三位创始人Kuleana取得了联系(Shigeta后来离开了公司)。
食物加速器的经历给了Prus一个在大学之前终身吃肉的基础，为替代蛋白质奠定了基础，并导致了关于如何开发更美味的鱼类替代品的思考。
普鲁斯说，Kuleana的金枪鱼是用铁和藻油以及不同蛋白质的混合物制成的，并以一种专有的方式形成。利用Good Seed Ventures最初提供的5万欧元种子资金，Kuleana正在开发一种脚手架，使该公司能够在不使用3D打印技术的情况下重现生鱼的味道和质地(普鲁斯表示，3D打印技术可以让产品看起来和尝起来都是“煮熟的”)。
为了进入市场，Kuleana正在联系全国各地餐厅的一些寿司厨师，因为第一个目标实际上是餐厅。
普鲁斯说：“我们发现，60%的[海鲜]是在户外食用的，与生海鲜一起食用的比例要高得多。”“我们在餐饮服务路线上做得更重了。”
今年内，Kuleana可能会作为寿司卷和戳碗中的金枪鱼替代品出现在一些商店，普鲁斯表示，这个价格已经可以与更高等级的金枪鱼腰肉竞争。
Kuleana已经在巴塞罗那和旧金山成功地进行了两次品尝测试，该公司在最近给投资者的一份报告中吹嘘说，已经收到了超过5万磅该公司素食金枪鱼替代品的订单意向书。
“金枪鱼的味道并不是最难的部分，…。这是质地的问题。“普鲁斯说。“(竞争对手)正在通过挤压来格式化他们的流程，这是行不通的。我相信，我们在生物技术方面所做的可能是最好的方法之一。“
note_en:
Now, a startup coming from Y Combinator’s summer cohort,  Kuleana, is hoping it can swim in those waters.
 While new businesses like Wild Type, Finless Foods, and Shiok Meats are all developing cell-based alternatives to using live tuna, salmon, and shrimp and Good Catch and Ocean Hugger are proposing their own tuna replacements, but Kuleana hopes to differentiate itself by replicating sushi-grade raw tuna.
  And that’s only the beginning, according to Kuleana co-founder and chief executive, Jacek Prus. Eventually, the company hopes to be making vegetarian alternatives to tuna, salmon, and more fish and shellfish.
 “Nobody has really done raw tuna very well yet,” says Jacek Prus. “ We’re going to do raw tuna and then probably salmon.”
 Prus became interested in the food industry after taking an animal ethics class at the University of Texas at Austin. After five years in the city, Prus moved to Europe and helped set up the  ProVeg International incubator.
 It was there that Prus got in touch with Ron Shigeta, a longtime researcher, technologist and entrepreneur in the food science space who previously worked as the Chief Science Officer at IndieBio. Shigeta put Prus in touch with the Barcelona-based food science researcher Sonia Hurtado and the three launched Kuleana (Shigeta has since left the company).
 The food accelerator experience gave Prus, a lifelong meat eater before college, a grounding in alternative proteins and led to the thinking around how to develop a more flavorful fish alternative.
  Kuleana’s tuna is made using iron and algae oil and a mix of different proteins, and forming them in a proprietary way, Prus said. Using an initial EUR50,000 in seed funding from Good Seed Ventures, Kuleana is developing a scaffolding that will enable the company to recreate the taste and texture of raw fish, without using 3D printing techniques (which Prus said could make the product look and taste “cooked”).
 To get to market Kuleana is reaching out to a number of sushi chefs at restaurants around the country, because the first target is, in fact, restaurants.
 “What we found is that 60 percent [of seafood] is eaten out of the home, and with raw seafood that’s significantly higher,” said Prus. “We’re doing it heavier on the food service route.”
 Within the year, Kuleana may be appearing in some stores as a tuna alternative in sushi rolls and poke bowls at a price that Prus said is already competitive with higher grade tuna loin.
 Kuleana already held two successful taste tests in Barcelona and San Francisco and the company boasted in a recent note to investors that there were letters of intent for orders of over 50,000 pounds of the company’s vegetarian tuna substitute.
 “The taste of tuna isn’t the hardest part… It’s the texture,” says Prus. “[Competitors] are formatting their process through extrusion and it will not work. I’m confident that what we’re doing with the biotech might be one of the best approaches out there.”
title: Y Combinator的Kuleana正在制作一种不含动物的生金枪鱼替代品
title_en: Y Combinator’s Kuleana is making an animal-free substitute for raw tuna
